message:
refactor(secrets/storage): deep cx threading through secret-scan checkpoint I/O (ft-xbnl0.2.3 tick 133)

Extends the ft-xbnl0.2.3 deep-cx-threading campaign into the secret-scan
incremental-resume path. Previously `scan_storage_incremental_with_cx`
announced itself as cx-first but still performed two storage calls —
`latest_secret_scan_report` (checkpoint read) and
`record_secret_scan_report` (checkpoint write) — through their legacy
non-cx entry points, which meant a cancelled cx could be silently
carried past a blocking I/O boundary.

Changes:
- frankenterm-core/storage.rs: add `latest_secret_scan_report_with_cx`
  and `record_secret_scan_report_with_cx` cx-first siblings. Each
  performs a `cx.checkpoint()` before delegating to its legacy
  counterpart, surfacing cancellation as a `StorageError::Database`
  with a descriptive message. Gated on `asupersync-runtime` to stay
  consistent with the rest of the cx-first API surface.
- frankenterm-core/secrets.rs: rewire
  `scan_storage_incremental_with_cx` to call the new
  `*_with_cx` variants for both checkpoint read and write, and update
  its doc comment to note the tick-133 deepening.

Keeps legacy non-cx methods intact so non-asupersync callers and older
code paths are unaffected; this is purely additive on the storage
layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
